title: Static vs Linked Memory Allocation
description: A flowchart comparing the allocation methods, access patterns, and memory efficiency of static arrays versus linked lists
elements: Linked List Nodes (Non-contiguous blocks with pointers)
relationships: Static allocation reserves fixed contiguous memory blocks at compile time
Linked allocation dynamically requests memory nodes from heap at runtime
Array elements are accessed via direct index calculation
List elements are accessed via sequential pointer traversal
Static arrays have fixed size while linked lists can grow/shrink dynamically
